mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, causing risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing construction activity accelerates when mortgage rates remain anchored near zero, signaling sustained aggregate demand expansion that lifts equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST rises alongside FEDFUNDS below 0.5%, or the conditional alpha vanishes after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflationary pressure, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in > 65% of months where CPI accelerates above 0.4%, or the signal's predictive power disappears after adjusting for realized volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum, supporting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of rolling windows, or the correlation between payroll growth and equity returns drops below 0.10 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and stagnant nonfarm payrolls indicates sector-specific supply bottlenecks rather than broad demand recovery, leading to earnings uncertainty and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal does not predict negative 20-day returns in > 50% of observed instances, or the interaction effect is fully absorbed by a single-factor market model.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% annualized rate signals an approaching recessionary threshold, causing risk aversion to spike and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.20 over a 5-year test period.
